jobs.description: (Budget is placeholder) We are building a real-time CFO + growth command center for a fast-scaling DTC subscription brand. This is not a basic dashboard project. The system must ingest data across our entire stack, normalize it into a single source of truth, and produce a daily executive briefing with actionable decisions, not just metrics. The end goal is a system that tells us what to do every day to scale to $1M/month revenue with strong cash efficiency and predictable growth. What We’re Building: A daily CFO tracker and decision engine that: Pulls data from all sources (APIs, exports, etc.) Calculates key business metrics (NCAC, MRR, churn, payback, cash runway) Outputs: Daily performance summary Financial health snapshot Clear, prioritized actions to take immediately This should function like an internal operating system for the business. Data Sources: You will be responsible for integrating and structuring data from: Shopify (API) Loop Subscriptions (API) Triple Whale Meta Ads (spend, CAC) Google Ads PayPal Finaloop (currently via exports, API coming) Mercury / bank balances Credit cards (balances + due dates) Meta + Google monthly invoicing (30-day terms) Melio (for extended float) Inventory system (Materials app – API + lead times) Core Metrics You Must Build + Maintain: Revenue (daily + rolling) Ad Spend (by channel) Blended ROAS NCAC (new customer acquisition cost) Cost to acquire a subscriber Subscription take rate Active subscribers MRR + MRR growth Churn (or leading indicators) LTV (modeled) Cash on hand Available credit + float Upcoming obligations (30-day window) Cash runway (in days) Inventory coverage (days remaining) System Requirements: Data Infrastructure Build a clean, reliable pipeline (no broken numbers) Recommend and implement the stack (BigQuery, Airtable, Sheets, etc.) Automate daily updates (no long-term manual work) CFO Logic Layer You will implement logic that: Evaluates unit economics (NCAC vs LTV) Tracks payback periods (target ≤ 60 days) Calculates real cash runway (including credit + float) Flags risks (cash, churn, inventory) Daily Output (Critical) System must generate a daily briefing including: Business health (growing / flat / declining) Key metric changes vs yesterday Cash position + runway Subscription health Ad performance diagnosis Inventory constraints Most important: Top 3 actions to take TODAY (required) Examples: Increase Meta spend from X → Y based on CAC stability Cut underperforming campaigns immediately Fix drop in subscription take rate with a specific intervention This is a decision engine, not a reporting dashboard. Who This Is For: You are: A senior data engineer or analytics operator Experienced with DTC, subscriptions, and paid media metrics Comfortable working with APIs, messy data, and financial logic Able to translate raw data into business decisions Bonus if you’ve worked with: Shopify + subscriptions (Loop, Recharge, etc.) Triple Whale or similar attribution tools CFO dashboards or financial modeling for ecommerce Who This Is NOT For: Basic dashboard builders Visualization-only specialists (Tableau, Looker, etc.) Anyone who needs heavy direction Success Criteria: Within 30–45 days: Fully automated daily dashboard Accurate financial and growth metrics Daily actionable briefing generated consistently Clear visibility into scaling decisions Compensation: Fixed + performance-based preferred Open to ongoing retainer if system proves valuable

jobs.description: We run a premium home goods Shopify store targeting US customers via Google Shopping ads exclusively. We have products live in Google Merchant Center with a Performance Max campaign running. The role is research and listing — find validated winning products, then upload and optimize them in Shopify. The Role This is a data-backed demand identification role. You find products that already have proven Google search demand, validate the numbers, and list them properly in Shopify. That is the job. We use Google Shopping as a demand capture channel — people are already searching for these products. Your research must prove that before anything gets listed. What You Will Do Find 5-10 product candidates daily using your research method of choice (Amazon, TikTok, Facebook ad library, competitor stores, product tools — anything works as long as you can back it with Google search data) Validate each with Google Trends (US, 12 months - 5 years) and Google Keyword Planner — exact monthly search volume and CPC required, not estimates Check Google Shopping competition for each product Source from AliExpress, CJ Dropshipping, or Alibaba — verify quality, confirm 3x margin minimum Write an optimized Google Merchant Center title for each candidate Upload approved products to Shopify — clean title, original description, premium AI images, correct GMC attributes, draft status Flag issues with existing products — pricing, titles, GMC compliance The Brand Premium, minimal, elevated home goods. Reference stores: Magnolia, McGee & Co, Ivory & Deene. Products should look at home in a well-designed apartment, not on Temu. Yes: woven baskets, linen bedding, velvet curtains, minimal lamps, textured throws, ceramic vases, bathroom rugs with a design angle. No: gadgets, novelty items, furniture, anything over 5 lbs, anything that looks cheap. Requirements Google Shopping research experience — you understand how demand capture works vs social ad product research Google Merchant Center familiarity — titles, image standards, disapproval reasons Shopify product listing experience — clean uploads, GMC-compliant setup Supplier sourcing experience — AliExpress, CJ, Alibaba Structured Google Sheets output — exact numbers, no vague descriptions To Apply — All Three Required Walk me through your exact step-by-step process for validating a product for Google Shopping. Be specific — which tools, what exact numbers you look for, what makes you say yes or no. Send a Loom video (5–10 minutes) showing your actual research workflow and results you have produced. — screen share, walk me through finding and validating one product in real time. Share one concrete example of a product you found that performed on Google Shopping — product name, the data behind it, what happened. Screenshots welcome. Answer all three in plain language. No bullet-point formatted AI responses — write how you actually talk. We can tell the difference. Rate: $5–10/hr depending on experience | Hours: 2–4/day to start | Trial task first | Ongoing if trial passes

jobs.description: We're a SaaS startup preparing to launch a cloud-based inventory management platform and need a thorough manual QA pass across the entire product — web app, native mobile apps (iOS + Android), and API — before launch. We have comprehensive test documentation ready: a 1,900-line onboarding doc with ~200+ test scenarios organized into prioritized suites, a companion manual tests appendix covering visual/cross-browser/accessibility/device testing, bug report templates, and daily reporting formats. You will not be guessing what to test — everything is documented with exact steps, expected results, and pre-seeded test accounts across four organizations at different plan tiers. About the platform: KeySpace is a B2B inventory, warehouse, and order management tool for small businesses. It includes integrations with Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, QuickBooks, Xero, Square, Etsy, BigCommerce, and eBay, plus demand forecasting, lot/expiration tracking, barcode scanning, role-based access, multi-org support, custom dashboards, billing via Stripe, and native iOS and Android apps. Scope of work: Web app: Full manual test pass across all features on a staging environment Test across desktop browsers: Chrome, Edge, Safari, Firefox Test responsive layouts at 375px / 768px / 1024px / 1280px / 1920px Test user flows end-to-end: signup, inventory lifecycle, orders, purchase orders, integrations, billing, role permissions, plan gating, multi-org switching Tenant isolation and security probing (cross-org data access, role enforcement at API level) Mobile apps (iOS + Android): Test all core flows on native apps: login, inventory browsing, barcode/QR scanning, adjustments, cycle counts, PO receiving, org switching Verify offline behavior (airplane mode toggle, cached data, mutation queuing) Test device-specific behavior: push notifications, camera permissions, deep linking, background kill/resume Platform-specific checks: iOS safe area/notch, Android back button, orientation lock API (via pre-built Postman collection — click Send, read the response): Tenant isolation verification at API level Role permission enforcement (403 responses for unauthorized actions) Plan gate enforcement (correct error shapes for gated features) Deliverables: Google Sheet test matrix updated in real time (one row per scenario, status + bug IDs + notes) Individual bug reports with steps to reproduce, expected vs. actual behavior, severity, platform/device, and screenshots or screen recordings Daily status report in a specified format every working day Engagement structure: 3–4 weeks. First 2 weeks are launch-critical. Weeks 3–4 are post-launch regression and patch verification. Remote, async. Work in your own timezone. $1,000 up front, $1,000 on delivery of completed test matrix meeting acceptance criteria. Acceptance criteria: 100% of P0 (critical) scenarios tested 80%+ of P1 scenarios tested Bugs reproducible from your report alone on first attempt (~90%) Daily report submitted every working day, no gaps Final sign-off summary reviewing open bugs, blocked scenarios, and overall quality assessment What we provide: Comprehensive onboarding document with all test scenarios, steps, and expected results Pre-seeded staging environment with four organizations across all plan tiers Test account credentials (web + mobile) TestFlight invite (iOS) and APK link (Android) for staging mobile builds Pre-built Postman collection for API scenarios Google Sheet test matrix ready to fill in Sandbox credentials for Shopify, Amazon, and QuickBooks integrations Ideal candidate: Experience testing SaaS web applications AND native mobile apps (iOS + Android) Comfortable with Postman (clicking Send on pre-built requests, reading responses — no authoring) Familiar with mobile-specific testing: camera permissions, offline behavior, deep linking, push notifications Owns at least one iPhone and one Android device for physical device testing Strong written communication for clear, reproducible bug reports Can work independently from detailed test documentation without hand-holding Ecommerce, inventory, or logistics tool experience a plus To apply, please include: 1–2 examples of past QA work or bug reports you've produced Confirmation you have access to both an iPhone and an Android device Your estimated timeline to complete the first P0 pass (we target 2 weeks) Any relevant experience with inventory, ecommerce, or Shopify-related tools

jobs.description: We’re looking for a Shopify Front-End Developer to optimize the user experience and improve conversion performance on our storefront. We are focused on improving how users interact with the site to increase conversion rate, reduce friction, and create a smoother shopping experience across devices, especially mobile. We’ve been actively analyzing user behavior through session recordings and have identified several areas of friction, including layout shifts (CLS issues), inconsistent rendering in in-app browsers (especially Instagram), image loading issues, and general UI/UX inconsistencies across product pages. The goal of this project is to diagnose and resolve these issues while improving overall site performance and usability. We are looking for someone who understands both Shopify development and conversion-focused UX. This includes improving page speed, stabilizing layout behavior, ensuring responsive design across devices, and optimizing the product page experience so users can move through the site without friction. We will provide access to our store, along with references and recordings to highlight the issues we are seeing. We are looking for someone who can not only implement fixes, but also identify additional areas of improvement based on best practices. This project will be milestone-based to ensure clarity and performance: 10% will be released after a full audit and diagnosis of issues, including a clear breakdown of the causes of layout shifts, rendering bugs, and UX friction points. 20% will be released after implementing fixes for layout stability (CLS), including preventing shifting elements, stabilizing image and media loading, and improving above-the-fold consistency. 25% will be released after resolving mobile and in-app browser issues, ensuring the site renders correctly within Instagram and other embedded browsers without breaking layout or spacing. 25% will be released after overall UX and performance improvements are implemented, including smoother scrolling, improved image handling, faster load times, and cleaner interaction across product pages. 20% will be released after final QA and testing across devices, with all fixes verified and no major visual or functional issues remaining. Our goal is not just to fix issues, but to build a stronger foundation for scaling traffic and improving conversion rates. If this project goes well, there will be opportunities for ongoing optimization work. Please include examples of Shopify stores you’ve worked on, specifically where you improved performance, UX, or conversion rate.
jobs.title: Shopify Front-End Developer

jobs.description: BEFORE YOU APPLY — You can train and maintain multiple consistent AI character models that look like the same person across dozens of different scenes, poses, and lighting conditions. Not "similar looking" — the same recognizable person every time. — You can take our real product photos and composite them into AI-generated scenes so the final image looks like a single professional photograph. —You can make hyper-realistic images where the people and the product look real and accurate — not AI-generated, not warped, not mismatched angles or lighting between the product/image. BONUS IF YOU DO VIDEO: — You can produce AI-generated video (not just stills) for short-form ad creative — models interacting with products, lifestyle scenes, scroll-stopping hooks. If your portfolio is random AI art with a different face in every image, this is not what we need. We need brand-consistent visual systems, not one-off generations. ABOUT US We're a hemp-derived cannabis and CBD ecommerce brand selling DTC through Shopify. Products include gummies, vapes, tinctures, and other hemp-derived items. ~80-person company, 7 years in business, self-funded. You'll work directly with our Channel Manager and CEO — no layers of middlemen. We review applications within 48 hours and move fast on the right fit. WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO This is an ongoing role, not a one-off project. You're building and maintaining our AI visual asset library and producing new creative on a regular cadence. Consistent Character Development & Maintenance Build and maintain multiple AI brand models — distinct, recognizable people who appear consistently across all our visual content. These aren't throwaway faces. They're recurring brand characters that customers will see across ads, product pages, emails, and social. You'll train custom models (LoRA, Dreambooth, or your preferred method) and maintain them as tools evolve. Product Compositing We'll provide high-quality photos of our actual products. You place them into AI-generated lifestyle scenes — held by our brand models, on tables, in environments — so the final output looks like a single cohesive professional photo or video. The product must look physically accurate: correct proportions, real label, proper lighting match. If the gummy jar looks AI-generated or the vape pen is warped, it doesn't ship. Photo Asset Production Lifestyle images for product pages (Shopify), ad creative for Meta and other platforms, email hero images, social content. High volume — we need someone who can produce batches efficiently, not someone who spends 4 hours on a single image. BONUS IF YOU CAN: Video Asset Production Short-form video for ad creative — 15 to 60 seconds. Brand models interacting with products, lifestyle scenes, scroll-stopping hooks. These feed directly into our paid media pipeline. Our media buying agency uses these assets to test and scale — so speed and volume matter alongside quality. Ad Creative Understanding You're not making art. You're making assets designed to stop thumbs and convert. You need to understand what makes a good hook frame, what compositions perform in feed ads, and how to produce creative variations for A/B testing. You'll work alongside our Performance Creative Designer and media buying agency — they'll give you briefs and performance feedback, and you'll iterate based on what's working. WHY THIS ISN'T A STANDARD AI ART GIG Realism and brand tone consistency are the hardest parts. Most AI creators can generate beautiful single images. Very few can maintain realism, style & model across 3-10 scenes. If you can't do this reliably, nothing else matters. Real product compositing separates professionals from prompters. Anyone can type "woman holding a product" into Midjourney. We need someone who can take our actual product photo and place it into an AI scene so seamlessly that you can't tell where the real photo ends and the AI begins. This requires Photoshop-level compositing skill on top of AI generation skill. Volume and speed matter. This feeds an active ad pipeline. We need someone who can deliver batches of assets on a weekly cadence, not someone who delivers 3 images a month. REQUIRED SKILLS (NON-NEGOTIABLE) — Custom AI model training for character consistency (LoRA, Dreambooth, IP-Adapter, or equivalent). You must be able to explain your method and show results. — Product compositing — placing real product photography into AI-generated scenes with matched lighting, perspective, and proportions. Photoshop proficiency is assumed. — Can produce at volume — weekly batches of photo and video assets, not artisanal one-offs. — Portfolio showing the realisim and same style and AI model consistently across multiple different scenes. This is the #1 filter. If your portfolio doesn't show this, don't apply. PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED — AI video generation using current tools (Runway, Kling, Veo, or equivalent). Must show examples. — Experience creating ad creative for cannabis, CBD, supplements, or other restricted-category brands — Understanding of Meta ad creative best practices (hook frames, aspect ratios, variation testing) TO APPLY — ANSWER THESE THREE SCREENING QUESTIONS AND INCLUDE YOUR PORTFOLIO RED FLAGS THAT WILL GET YOU SCREENED OUT — Portfolio full of random AI art with different faces in every image — tells us you can prompt but can't maintain consistency — No product compositing examples — tells us you've never worked with real product photography — No video examples — we need both photo and video, not one or the other — "I can learn any tool" without showing current proficiency — we're paying for expertise, not a learning curve — Application that doesn't answer the three questions above — tells us you didn't read the post

Maintain strong follow-up cadence to maximise conversion rates - Handle objections effectively and close deals with confidence - Manage and optimise pipeline activity within CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) #### Business Development (Growth & Expansion) - Identify new product opportunities, market gaps, and revenue streams - Contribute to pricing strategy, positioning, and go-to-market initiatives - Support expansion into new markets and channels --- ### Requirements (Essential) - Proven experience in sales AND business development - Strong track record in high-ticket or consultative sales - Confident communicator with excellent phone and presentation skills - Exceptional English communication (written and verbal) - Demonstrated ability to generate your own pipeline (not just close inbound leads) - Strong commercial acumen and understanding of sales funnels - Highly organised, proactive, and results-driven - Able to work consistently within New Zealand business hours --- ### Application Requirement Please include the following in your application: - Overview of your sales and business development experience - Examples of high-ticket products/services you have sold (including price ranges) - Experience generating your own leads or partnerships - A recent example of a deal you originated and closed - Your approach to building and managing a sales pipeline --- ### Preferred Experience - Experience in wellness, fitness, lifestyle, or premium product industries - Experience working with B2B partnerships or commercial clients - Familiarity with CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) - Experience with eCommerce platforms (e.g., Shopify) - Background in scaling or growing a business unit or territory --- ### What We Offer - Flexible remote working environment - Opportunity to grow into a senior leadership role as the business scales - Work within a premium, fast-growing wellness brand - Commission and performance-based incentives - High ownership role with real impact on business growth --- ### Who This Role Suits This role is best suited for someone who: - Is both a closer and a builder - Thrives on driving revenue and creating new opportunities - Takes ownership and operates with a high level of autonomy - Thinks commercially and strategically, not just tactically - Wants to play a key role in scaling a business --- If you are an experienced sales professional who can both close deals and build new growth channels, we’d like to hear from you.

jobs.description: I'm building a consumer app in a $50B+ market that's massively underserved by technology. The product is 85% built and I'm looking for a developer or small technical team to take it from prototype to published. This isn't a concept or a pitch deck — the app is functional today with a working UI, live payment system, and AI integration. I need someone who can bring it to production quality and get it into the App Store and Google Play. What I need: → React / React Native / JSX fluency — the entire codebase is React. Not looking for someone who's learning it. → App Store + Google Play publishing experience — you've submitted apps, dealt with the review process, and know the compliance requirements for both platforms. → Payment infrastructure — Stripe is already integrated. I need someone who understands subscription lifecycles, webhook handling, failed payments, upgrades/downgrades, and App Store payment policy compliance. → Backend architecture for scale — the app needs to support 10,000+ users with real-time data, image storage, analytics ingestion, background jobs, and scheduled automations. You should be comfortable with database optimization, caching, rate limiting, and job queues. → AI/LLM integration — the app uses AI for image recognition and data extraction. Experience with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, or computer vision is a strong plus. → Data pipeline experience — the platform ingests and structures large volumes of data from external sources. Experience building reliable automated ingestion systems matters.
jobs.title: Website/App Developer
